title: podman push uploads every layer every time
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

"podman push" will push layers already in the destination repository

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Prepare for an insecure local registry:
edit /etc/containers/registries.conf to include the following
```
[registries.insecure]
registries = ['localhost:5000']
```

2. Install and run docker-distribution locally:
```
dnf install docker-distribution
systemctl start docker-distribution
```

3. Pull and tag some multi-layer images which have common layers:
```
podman pull docker.io/tripleomaster/centos-binary-heat-engine:current-tripleo
podman pull docker.io/tripleomaster/centos-binary-heat-api:current-tripleo
podman tag docker.io/tripleomaster/centos-binary-heat-api:current-tripleo localhost:5000/tripleomaster/centos-binary-heat-api:current-tripleo
podman tag docker.io/tripleomaster/centos-binary-heat-engine:current-tripleo localhost:5000/tripleomaster/centos-binary-heat-engine:current-tripleo
```

4. Push to the local repository (duplicate push is deliberate)
```
podman push localhost:5000/tripleomaster/centos-binary-heat-api:current-tripleo
podman push localhost:5000/tripleomaster/centos-binary-heat-api:current-tripleo
podman push localhost:5000/tripleomaster/centos-binary-heat-engine:current-tripleo
```

**Describe the results you received:**
Every layer was pushed again each time "podman push" was run

**Describe the results you expected:**
The second push of centos-binary-heat-api should have skipped pushing every layer. The push of centos-binary-heat-engine should have skipped most layers, just like it did for "podman pull"

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**
podman version 0.8.1

**Output of `podman info`:**
host:
  MemFree: 340643840
  MemTotal: 12276117504
  SwapFree: 5950140416
  SwapTotal: 6174011392
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  hostname: sbaker-t540s
  kernel: 4.17.11-200.fc28.x86_64
  os: linux
  uptime: 50h 36m 20.13s (Approximately 2.08 days)
insecure registries:
  registries:
  - localhost:5000
  - 192.168.20.11:8787
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mountopt=nodev
  - overlay.override_kernel_check=true
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 6
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
In TripleO we have about 100 images which share a lot of layers. We serve these from a registry available to the cluster. With this number of images, populating this registry needs to be as efficient as possible. Since "docker push" handles this deduplication, switching to podman or skopeo would be quite a performance hit for us.

If I understand [this skopeo RFE](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/395) correctly, containers-storage -> registry de-duplication should be possible in a higher level tool like podman because it has access to the compressed and uncompressed layer hashes.

comments.body: @umohnani8 in your work with the copy functions, have you seen anywhere where we can deal with this sort of caching.  lets discuss tomorrow.
@mtrmac As the person who wrote the c/image APIs, any pointers here?

I've been looking into the REST API required for the 2 cases above.
For uploading the same image twice, before a layer is put, a HEAD /v2/namespace/image-1/blobs/sha256:... is made first, and upload only occurs if there is a 404 returned.

For uploading a layer which is shared by another image, when the HEAD returns a 404, the next request is a POST /v2/namespace/image-2/blobs/uploads/?from=namespace%2Fimage-1&mount=sha256%3A...

The first case seems fairly trivial, the second case would require querying the containers-storage layer for other images which use this layer (then maybe some extra HEAD calls to see if that layer is in the registry under any other known images)

See Cross Repository Blob Mount in the [registry spec](https://docs.docker.com/registry/spec/api/#content-digests)

Three separate aspects to this:

1. Not copying anything about the image when the image is present at the destination ( https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/395 ). That should be fairly easy, and higher-level callers can do the check ~independently (like CRI-O does already). The trade-off is that the “is the image present” check itself is a bit expensive, several round-trips, and there’s not _that much_ reason to expect that this will be a net benefit.

2. Not re-pushing layers when the source is uncompressed and we are compressing on the fly ( https://github.com/containers/image/issues/475 ). This is the most important possible improvement, and most complex: it requires $something to store a long-term mapping between DiffID and several possible known digests (and probably their “locations” in different registries/repoistories).

3. Support of cross-repository mounts within a destination registry, if the blob is known to be present in a different repository on the same server ( https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/274 ). Basically a corner case of the previous case.

I think ideally we should have 2.+3., and probably not 1.  2.+3. either costs ~nothing (when the local storage does not have any record of the layer existing on the destination), or it is almost certainly a _big_ improvement (when we do have a local record of the layer existing on the destination in the past, it is quite unlikely to have been removed in the meantime—most registries do not support deletion of any kind at all).

If we have 2.+3., the cost to unnecessarily re-copy an image (possibly prevented by 1.) is a few round-trips, so 1. is much less noticeable, and the expected frequency of copying an already-available image would have to be pretty high for 1. to be useful on average.
@mtrmac SGTM, could you implement something like this?
Not right this minute, let’s add it to the backlog.
I believe this is fixed in latest podman.

Was this fixed?

@kbaegis, the output indicates that it's an old version of Podman. Can you check the version and re-try with a more recent one?
